other_observers: none; I am the original finder.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch very close to the RMBO Bird Banding Station.
description: A medium sized flycatcher the size of Western Wood-Pewee which was present at the same location. The bird was almost entirely identified by the song. I did get a good scope view of the bill however from underneath, so the comments are about the mandible and the underneath parts of the mandible. The mandible was almost entirely bright orange, with a distinct darker tip, the was about 1/6 or 1/7 the lenght of the bill, i.e. only a tiny bit of the tip was dusky in appearance. The bird had two whitish wingbars, no eyering, a crest, a long tail and was seen many times in an upright posture. When visible, it was always perched on a dead stick, usually inside the canopy of a live tree, but at times near the top of a dead snag that still had most of its finer branches on it.   I did study the vest on the bird's breast and to me it was noticeably lighter than the typical CO Western Wood-Pewees. I saw its back briefly and it appeared lighter overall than the nearby Western Wood-Pewees.Behaviors: Very active, but didn't see it actively foraging. Mostly changed perches every 4 or 5 seconds, returning after some time to the same tree, but never seemingly to the same perch. It sang on landing on new perches and the perches were as low as 3 feet off the ground (once) to over 60 feet high. Mostly perched from mid-storey to upper levels of the tree.   On four occasions the bird interacted with Western Wood-Pewees, whereas the Western would chase the Eastern off of the selected perch. The Eastern interacted on three occasions with the same Western, but it also interacted on separate occasions with as many as three different Western Wood-Pewees.Call: Yes,  This bird sang, almost continuously for a little over an hour. It sang the classic Eastern Wood-Pewee song, i.e. a plaintive drawn out, "pee-a-wee" and about 10 percent of the time it also sang, always after the "pee-a-wee" song and then about 3 seconds later a downslurred, two-not "pee-you".Plumage: unknown
